sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, showing inconsistency in defense with no clean sheets in 10 games. FCV Dender, on the other hand, are struggling, securing just 1 win in their last 5 matches, with a tendency for low-scoring games (under 2.5 goals in 5 of 6 recent matches). Waregem's attacking output, led by Jeppe Erenbjerg (8 goals), gives them an edge over Dender's less potent attack.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, SV Zulte Waregem have remained unbeaten against FCV Dender, with a win (2-1) and a draw (2-2). Both teams have scored in 4 of their last 5 head-to-heads, indicating competitive matches with defensive vulnerabilities on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem sit 12th in the league with 26 points, having scored 31 goals (5th in the league) but conceding 35 (12th worst). FCV Dender, languishing in 16th with 17 points, have managed only 18 goals while conceding 36, highlighting their struggle to balance attack and defense. Waregem's higher conversion rate and fast breaks could exploit Dender's defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Waregem, Jeppe Erenbjerg's goal-scoring form (8 goals, 3rd in the league) will be crucial, while Anosike Ementa’s ability to draw fouls could create opportunities. FCV Dender rely on Bruny Nsimba (7 goals, 5th in the league), who has been a standout in recent head-to-heads with an average Sofascore rating of 7.4.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction (50% home win, 28% draw, 22% away win) and Waregem's superior league standing and attacking stats, they are favorites to win at Elindus Arena. Dender's poor away form and inability to score consistently may hinder their chances, though both teams' defensive issues suggest goals could come at both ends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between SV Zulte Waregem and FCV Dender ended in a goalless draw, with neither team managing to break the deadlock. Zulte Waregem showed more dominance in possession and created a few promising opportunities, including a big chance, though they failed to convert. FCV Dender, on the other hand, struggled to create significant openings but managed to get a couple of shots on target. Discipline was an issue for the away side, as they picked up multiple yellow cards compared to just one for the home team. Overall, Zulte Waregem appeared more likely to score but lacked the clinical edge needed in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Zulte Waregem held the upper hand in terms of attacking momentum for the majority of the time. They started strongly with consistent pressure in the early stages, pushing forward with intent in multiple spells. Their momentum dipped briefly around the midway point, allowing FCV Dender to gain some ground with a notable attacking phase. However, Zulte Waregem regained control soon after with renewed vigor in their forward play. Towards the end of the half, Dender mounted a significant push, showing a shift in momentum with several threatening moves, but they couldn’t sustain it as the half concluded with Zulte Waregem back on the front foot.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Zulte Waregem's performance in the first half was marked by a slight edge in ball possession and a higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area, showcasing their intent to attack. They also dominated in duels, winning a significant percentage of both ground and aerial challenges, which helped them maintain control. Despite creating a big chance, their inability to register shots on target remains a concern. FCV Dender, in contrast, struggled with duels and aerial battles, losing out in most physical encounters. However, they managed to test the goalkeeper with a couple of efforts on target, even if their overall threat was limited due to fewer entries into dangerous areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Zulte Waregem, Joseph Opoku emerged as a key figure in the first half, heavily involved in creating opportunities and posing a threat in the attacking third, though he missed a big chance. Jeppe Erenbjerg also stood out with his creativity, contributing to dangerous moves for his side. On the FCV Dender side, Bryan Goncalves made a notable impact defensively while also pushing forward at times, showing versatility. Fabio Ferraro and Bruny Nsimba offered some threat with their attempts on goal, giving Dender a glimmer of hope in an otherwise subdued attacking display.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Zulte Waregem are likely to continue their dominance in possession and push for a breakthrough, especially given their higher expected goals in the first half. They will need to sharpen their finishing to capitalize on the chances they create. FCV Dender, meanwhile, may adopt a more defensive approach to protect their clean sheet while looking for counter-attacking opportunities. Their discipline will need to improve to avoid further cards that could disrupt their game plan. A goal seems more probable for the home side, but Dender’s resilience could make it a tight contest if they tighten up at the back.
